system.description: The deeper descent from the bioluminescent grotto into Rot-infected passages. The party must navigate increasingly corrupted tunnels where the Root-Runes on the walls are blackened and the stone itself shows veins of dark corruption. Kaelen can read the Rot's progress. Getting through requires careful movement, Rot-avoidance, and supporting Kaelen as she processes increasingly disturbing information.

complications.round1:
The passages below the grotto are Rot-infected — black veins run through the stone, Root-Runes are screaming silently. Kaelen slows to read them, increasingly disturbed. The air is wrong. The party must navigate while keeping Kaelen moving and protecting her focus.

  • Intuition: Detect which tunnel sections are structurally compromised by Rot corruption vs. which are merely discolored (collapsing tunnels are a risk).
  • Reason: Help Kaelen interpret what she's reading on the walls: "Yggdrasil's roots are literally rotting." Assist with her analysis so she doesn't fall behind.
  • Might: Clear Rot-bloated fungal growth blocking passage entrances (the growth is not aggressive but is dense).
  • Presence: Keep the group moving when the oppressive wrongness of the passage makes heroes want to stop and turn back.
complications.round2:
The final section of passage is choked with Rot-Echoes — not yet combat-triggering, but actively trying to find warm flesh. The party must avoid triggering a combat encounter while getting everyone through.

  • Agility: Move silently through a section where Rot-Echo clusters hover near the ceiling (disturbing them triggers combat).
  • Intuition: Identify the Rot-Echoes' patrol pattern and find gaps in their movement.
  • Reason: Recall whether Root-Rune symbols on the passage walls can be activated to temporarily suppress Rot-Echoes (they can — moderate DC).
  • Presence: Keep a frightened hero from making noise as they pass through the cluster.

outcomes.round1:
Total Success: The party moves efficiently. Kaelen completes her reading and delivers her conclusion: "If the dead can think, they can plan. Something intelligent is organizing them." No structural collapses. The party reaches the exit passage ahead of schedule.
Partial Success: Progress made but one structural collapse forces a detour. Each hero takes 3 damage from falling stone dust and debris. Kaelen's analysis is incomplete — she knows something directs the dead but not more.
Failure: A Rot-weakened section of tunnel collapses. The party must dig through or backtrack (1 hour delay). Each hero takes 6 damage. Two heroes gain the weakened condition until they take a breather.
outcomes.round2:
Total Success: The party slips through without triggering a single Rot-Echo. They emerge into the exit passage clear and undetected. Kaelen notes: "The Rot is getting thicker this direction. We're moving toward the source."
Partial Success: One Rot-Echo is disturbed and attaches briefly to a hero (4 cold damage) before being shaken off. The rest pass safely.
Failure: The Rot-Echoes swarm — combat triggers immediately. 3 Rot-Echoes + 1 Barrow-Draugr (drawn by the Echoes' agitation).
